title: chelyella bracteata
intro: Chelyella bracteata, also known as the "milk thistle," is a biennial or perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating liver and gallbladder issues due to its high concentration of silymarin, a flavonoid complex. The silymarin in Chelyella bracteata has been shown to have numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving antioxidant defenses, and protecting against liver damage. Chelyella bracteata is often prepared as a herbal supplement in capsule or tablet form, or as a tea, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
